summary: TD's plan is structurally sound. Discovery dispatched both high-importance IPs (IP1 state transport, IP2 push delivery) to Researcher; Probe 001 returned 6 verbatim citations from canonical sources covering GitHub Pages, Firestore, Apple WebKit Web Push, and Supabase auto-pause. TD sourced every external-system claim from canonical_evidence with citations_pointer and used td_plan only for internal UX/structure choices, which is correct discipline under Principle H. All 8 FC requirements have matching DCA assertions, and PNV.1 is a production-fidelity 2-profile play-through that literally exercises the prompt verb 'play (asynchronous head-to-head GTO duel)' end-to-end. Two structural lifecycle gaps in the proper-noun ledger (status fields never transitioned, no demotion-v{N}.json artifacts) are flagged below as low/medium because the substantive content (TD's citations for PN.2 and TD's reliance on the in-ledger demotion_note for PN.1) is present; the gaps are in Discovery's record-keeping, not in TD's compliance. The 'fully hostable on GitHub Pages' constraint is interpreted honestly — the deliverable itself is a static directory served by GitHub Pages, and Firebase is a free-tier backend the app calls (similar to any static SPA calling a third-party API). The setup burden is acknowledged in S6.A4 (README) and S5.A5 (onboarding copy) but the first-contact narrative could be tightened.

findings.description: PN.2 'GitHub Pages' is marked role: target_defining with verification_status: 'pending'. TD's downstream artifacts (probe-001 with CIT.1 verbatim_excerpt + DCA.32, DCA.37, IP1.A1) treat it as effectively verified via canonical evidence, and the citation chain is intact. Structurally, however, the ledger's verification_status was never transitioned to 'verified' to reflect that Researcher resolved it. This is a Discovery record-keeping gap, not a TD compliance gap: TD did dispatch a Researcher probe and TD's sourcing meets Principle E. Flag is low-severity and structural-only.
findings.recommended_action: Discovery may update PN.2.verification_status to 'verified' and add citations[] pointer to research/probes/probe-001/findings.json#citations[id=CIT.1] in a ledger-v2 amendment. Non-blocking; TD's downstream artifacts already cite the verbatim excerpt where it matters.

findings.description: PN.1 'GTO Poker Training' is role: supportive with canonical_source_required: false but verification_status remains 'pending' and the four-guardrail rationale lives in an inline demotion_note rather than a separate decisions/discovery/demotion-v{N}.json record. The substance is fully documented (all four demotion guardrails — supportive role, internal ancestor not external system, telos expressible without it, no runtime linkage — are stated in demotion_note and reinforced in A20 and DCA.31/DCA.54). The structural gap is the absence of a status transition to 'demoted' and a standalone demotion artifact.
findings.recommended_action: Discovery may transition PN.1.verification_status to 'demoted' in a ledger-v2 amendment and emit a demotion-v1.json record citing the existing demotion_note text. Non-blocking; TD's plan correctly treats PN.1 as a non-target supportive noun (DCA.31 enforces that the new artifact does NOT present itself as the ancestor).

findings.description: FC.2 says 'no procedural setup required between landing and starting a game'. DCA.34 mirrors this and parenthetically adds 'assuming the user has already configured Firebase'. The four-step user onboarding (create Firebase project, paste config values, push to GitHub, enable Pages) is a real first-contact burden that lives BEFORE the published URL exists. The architecture is correct (it's the minimum-feasible cost of true out-of-app push on a static host per Probe 001), but the first-contact narrative could be tightened: the README (S6.A4) and inline onboarding copy (S5.A5) need to be load-bearing for the 'feels like a finished product' deliverability target, especially given the user's intended audience (his mom). This is not a coverage gap in TD's plan — both S5.A5 and S6.A4 exist and are asserted — but the severity of the dependency warrants explicit deliverability emphasis in the Build/Verifier phases.
findings.recommended_action: During Build/Verification, require that the README's four-step setup be (a) ordered, (b) screenshot-illustrated where it crosses the Firebase console, and (c) include a 'paste this exact JSON shape' template for the config file. Verifier should treat README onboarding clarity as a load-bearing acceptance criterion (production-fidelity 'a non-developer can follow this' read-through), not a perfunctory presence check.

findings.description: Per Discovery's concerns_for_editor[0], the user's emotional context (playing with his mom long-distance) is load-bearing for deliverable value, not decoration. The telos and PNV.1 exercise the mechanical play loop end-to-end but do not specifically test the wrap-up reveal experience or the per-scenario note affordance as the value-defining moments. DCA.10 (joint-confidence-min ordering) and DCA.12 (per-scenario notes round-tripped) cover the mechanics, and IP7.A2 + S4.A3 cover the reveal panel structurally. No assertion is missing; the flag is that the verbal/textual quality of the reveal copy and the note round-trip (which is what makes the app 'feel like' the prior app) is not explicitly named as a deliverability criterion.
findings.recommended_action: Verifier should weight the wrap-up screen's joint-confidence-min disagreement list and the per-scenario note reveal as deliverability-defining acceptance — not just 'list renders' but 'list reads as a conversation prompt the way the prior app's data summary did'. This is a copy/UX quality check, not a structural one, and lives in the Verifier's production-fidelity exercise.

recommendations: Treat the README onboarding (S6.A4, DCA.21) and the inline iOS Add-to-Home-Screen copy (S5.A5) as load-bearing deliverability artifacts. The 'fully hostable on GitHub Pages' constraint is honestly satisfied — Firebase is a backend service the app calls, not a self-hosted server — but the four-step setup IS the user's first-contact experience and must be polished accordingly. Recommend Verifier do an end-to-end 'non-developer follows the README' read-through as part of production fidelity.

Discovery may emit a ledger-v2 amendment to transition PN.2 verification_status: pending -> verified (citing probe-001 CIT.1) and PN.1 verification_status: pending -> demoted with a demotion-v1.json artifact preserving the existing demotion_note text. This is record-keeping cleanup, not a substantive change; non-blocking for Coordinator's first wave.

Verifier should explicitly assert that the wrap-up screen's highest-joint-confidence-disagreements list and the per-scenario note reveal feel conversation-provoking, not just structurally present. The user's anecdote (mom and son talking through hands) is the load-bearing emotional value Discovery flagged; structural assertions S4.A3, S4.A7, DCA.10, DCA.12 cover the mechanics but not the experiential quality.

Pick the new artifact's display name now (DCA.31 enforces it is NOT 'GTO Poker Training') so Build does not block on naming. Candidates already noted in TD's concerns_for_editor: 'GTO Duel', 'GTO Pair', 'GTO Head-to-Head'. Recommend 'GTO Duel' for tightness.
